meta.description: Boxing venues located in the USA, covering all domestic circuit tiers. Small gym venues for club-level competition, regional sports arenas for regional tournaments, and major national arenas for the National Championship and Golden Gloves. Historic venues like Kronk Gym and Gleason's are included at smaller capacity — their prestige is cultural, not architectural. Venue ids share a global namespace — all venue files are loaded together and ids must be unique across the set.

venues.name: Brownsville Boxing Club
venues.description: A working boxing gym in Brownsville, Brooklyn. Club shows here are fought in the most concentrated boxing neighbourhood in American history. The building has seen it all — debut bouts, local feuds, coaches who went on to train world champions. You can feel the weight of the place.

venues.name: Gleason's Gym
venues.description: The oldest boxing gym in New York, operating since 1937. Gleason's has trained Muhammad Ali, Jake LaMotta, Roberto Duran, and hundreds more. Located under the Brooklyn Bridge in DUMBO, it hosts club-level amateur shows alongside its professional training operations. A living museum of American boxing.

venues.name: Kronk Gym
venues.description: Emanuel Steward's gym on McGraw Avenue in Detroit — the birthplace of the Kronk style. Thomas Hearns turned professional here. Lennox Lewis, Julio César Chávez Jr., and dozens of world champions trained in this building. The gym hosts club-level events but its significance dwarfs its capacity. There are gyms in American boxing and there is Kronk.

venues.name: Police Athletic League Gym — Philadelphia
venues.description: The PAL gym in North Philadelphia, one of the foundational institutions of Philly boxing. Club shows here are entry-level but the standard is high — coaches who trained Bernard Hopkins have walked through this building. The ring sits in a converted community centre that smells like history.

venues.name: Lowell Boxing Club
venues.description: The main boxing club in Lowell, Massachusetts — a city that produced Micky Ward and carries the boxing culture in its working-class Irish, Cambodian, and Latino communities. Small shows here draw loyal crowds from the mill town neighbourhoods.

venues.name: Chicago PAL Boxing
venues.description: The Police Athletic League boxing programme in Chicago, operating across multiple gym facilities on the south and west sides. Club events draw fighters from across the city's neighbourhood gym network.

venues.name: Houston Boxing Academy
venues.description: The main amateur boxing facility in Houston, operating as a community programme with youth development at its core. Club shows here draw from the city's Mexican-American and African-American communities.

venues.name: Wintrust Arena
venues.description: Chicago's mid-size multipurpose arena, used for boxing cards, basketball, and major events. Regularly hosts regional boxing tournaments and Golden Gloves Chicago regional rounds.

venues.name: Freeman Coliseum
venues.description: The major arena in San Antonio, used for boxing cards since the 1950s. Bob Arum has staged cards here; the city's boxing community turns out for major events. Regional tournament host for the Southwest circuit.

venues.name: Toyota Center
venues.description: Houston's major arena and one of the larger boxing venues in Texas. Has hosted major title fights. Regional tournament host when the Southwest circuit comes to Houston.

venues.name: MGM Grand Garden Arena
venues.description: The premier boxing venue in America. More world title fights have been staged here than at any other single venue in history. When something matters in professional boxing, it is usually happening at the MGM Grand. Amateur boxing at this level is the rare national championship event that warrants the Strip.

venues.name: Madison Square Garden
venues.description: The World's Most Famous Arena. Madison Square Garden has hosted championship boxing since 1879. Joe Louis, Sugar Ray Robinson, Willie Pep, Rocky Marciano, Muhammad Ali — they all fought here. The Golden Gloves New York finals have been held at MSG for decades. There is no building in American boxing with more history.

venues.name: Crypto.com Arena
venues.description: The Staples Center by any other name. Los Angeles's major multipurpose arena has hosted Oscar De La Hoya title fights, Manny Pacquiao appearances, and numerous world championship events. The National Championship and Golden Gloves West Coast finals are staged here when the tournament rotates to Los Angeles.

venues.name: Wells Fargo Center
venues.description: Philadelphia's major arena. The city that gave boxing the Philly shell deserves a proper venue — Wells Fargo Center hosts major boxing cards and serves as the National Championship host when the tournament rotates to Philadelphia.

venues.name: Little Caesars Arena
venues.description: Detroit's main multipurpose arena. When a major boxing event comes to the Motor City — and they do come, because this is still a boxing city — this is where it happens. National Championship host when the tournament comes to Detroit.
